what_it_does: React region component that displays backend-composed help for the currently indicated ui:// element. It renders a plain-language lead (what it is / how to use it), a collapsible how-to-change drill-down with file scope and blast-radius summary, a learned-presentation-preference marker, and an in-panel ShapeHow feedback affordance. It returns null when no ui:// element is indicated.
observations: Consumes addressHelp, addressHelpBusy, addressHelpError, prefBusy, and setPresentationPrefAt from AppContext rather than composing help locally.
Renders five distinct states: all legs present, thin howto, no-code scope, well-formed-but-unregistered, and malformed/fail-loud error.
Carries data-ui-ref="ui://inspector/help" on every render branch so the help panel is itself a guidable corpus element.
The mechanism drill-down is closed by default but can be hoisted open when a learned pref requests lead_with:change.
Blast radius is summarized by kind (co-reference, dependents, dependencies, semantic neighbours) with collapsible member lists.
ShapeHow is embedded inside the panel to let operators set a presentation preference, refetch, and see the adapted re-render.
shortAddr strips the ui:// prefix for a calmer kicker while the full address remains in the SectionHead tag.
representsLabel extracts a feature-id from the represents string to use as the only available WHAT when no howto is authored.
A Badge shows present/3 to give an at-a-glance completeness cue.
standouts: The altitude move: plain-language lead up front, mechanism demoted to an on-demand drill-down.
Model-free structural adapt: lead_with:change hoists the mechanism section open deterministically.
Self-referential data-ui-ref makes the help surface help-able.
Degrade-clean handling of missing howto, no-code scope, unregistered addresses, and backend errors.
conventions: Uses shared kit components (SectionHead, Badge, Surface, EmptyState) and design-token styling via var().
Functional React components with TypeScript; local state reset per locus via useEffect.
File-local, no ranking; comments written in a calm-authority display voice.
data-ui-ref attribute follows the corpus addressing convention.
Registry-is-truth: does not recompose backend bundle in the frontend.
concerns: BlastReach and ReachKind use any for radius and tone, weakening type safety.
representsLabel relies on a regex over an em-dash and 'represents', which is brittle if corpus formatting changes.
Assumes change and change.scope exist whenever legs.how_to_change is true, with no visible runtime guards.
The lead_with change detection normalizes pref.arg against a hardcoded list of strings.
The deterministic symbol 'map' is not a declared file symbol but an Array.prototype.map call.
notes: A right-rail help surface that translates backend address help into operator-altitude prose, hides the mechanism behind a caret, and lets operators teach the system how to present it.
questions: What is the exact runtime shape of the addressHelp bundle (legs_present, how_to_change, blast_radius fields)?
How does setPresentationPrefAt correlate the indicated address with the pref and trigger a refetch?
What other presentation_pref kinds and directives exist beyond lead_with and terser/more/shape?
Can the blast_radius be absent or malformed, and how does the backend signal stale or empty reach?
What props and rendering behavior does ShapeHow expose beyond current, busy, and onShape?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is an altitude-transformed address-help surface that reads a backend bundle, renders distinct degrade-clean states, and adds a feedback loop for presentation preferences.
intention_signals: Make backend help intelligible to a non-technical operator by leading with prose, hiding implementation details until asked, honestly surfacing missing corpus data, and letting operators shape how the help is shown.
novelty: Combines a learned-presentation-preference marker, a deterministic model-free structural adapt that hoists the mechanism drill-down, and an embedded feedback affordance so the panel can reshape its own rendering.
contribution: Provides a concrete UI region that consumes composed address-help bundles, separates plain-language guidance from implementation details via a collapsible drill-down, handles five empty/error states without blanking, exposes its own ui:// address for guidance, and integrates an in-panel feedback loop for presentation preferences.
summary_for_embedding: AddressHelp.tsx is a React right-rail region that renders backend-composed help for an indicated ui:// element, with altitude-layered prose, a collapsible how-to-change drill-down, blast-radius summary, degrade-clean empty/error states, a self-referential data-ui-ref, and a ShapeHow feedback affordance for learned presentation preferences.
intention_for_embedding: To surface address help at a non-technical operator's altitude while honestly degrading on missing corpus data and letting operators shape how the help is presented.

symbols.does: Strips the leading ui:// prefix from an address.
symbols.description: Utility function that removes the ui:// scheme from an indicated address to produce a calmer kicker string, while the full address remains visible in the SectionHead tag context.

symbols.does: Extracts a feature-id from a represents-style description string.
symbols.description: Parses a what_this_is string of the form 'ui://x — represents FEAT-id' and returns the feature-id, used as the registry-identity fallback when no authored how-to prose exists.

symbols.does: Main region component that renders composed address help or null.
symbols.description: Conditionally returns null for non-ui:// loci; otherwise renders loading, error, unregistered, thin-howto, or full help states, including a learned-pref marker, a toggleable mechanism drill-down, a blast-radius summary, and the ShapeHow feedback control.

symbols.does: Renders the file scope list via Array.prototype.map.
symbols.description: Built-in array method invocation over change.scope that emits a Surface-wrapped code element for each file in the how-to-change scope; listed as a deterministic symbol but is a method call rather than a file-defined symbol.

Component that takes a radius object and renders collapsible count chips for co-references, structural dependents, structural dependencies, and semantic neighbours, degrading cleanly with a note when the radius is empty or stale.

symbols.does: Renders one kind of reach as a collapsible header plus members.
symbols.description: Component that displays a single blast-radius kind as a caret-button header with a label and count Badge, expanding inline to show the list of member code symbols when opened.
